original_abstract: International Higher Education (IHE) provides high-level commentary and rigorous analysis on the evolving trends and critical challenges facing global higher education. This publication serves as a vital resource for institutions and stakeholders, exploring the complexities of international collaboration—ranging from the logistical hurdles of disparate regulatory systems and credit frameworks to the "vexing" debates surrounding the legitimacy and accreditation of joint and double degrees. By examining the impact of shifting student mobility patterns and the essential role of the academic profession, IHE offers the nuanced perspective necessary to navigate the increasingly interconnected global academic landscape.
humanized_abstract: International Higher Education (IHE) offers brief incisive analysis of select issues of concern to institutions and others involved in international higher education. The magazine delves into the issues facing international higher education, examining their complexity and looking for perspective in what is often a confusing and dynamic environment. Issues include analysis of the practical difficulties involved in collaborative degree programs — from dealing with differing regulations and systems of credit to the vexing issues of legitimacy and proper accreditation of joint and double degrees. IHE also examines the impact of the changing dynamics of student mobility and the role of academics in a rapidly changing global higher education system.
